name: Attio
description: A modern CRM and workspace for managing relationships, contacts, and company data with a programmable API.

auth_methods.note: Attio's official Authentication docs state that interacting with the Attio API requires an Attio API key (sent as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`), and also document OAuth 2.0 for integrations across multiple workspaces (access tokens also sent via the Bearer Authorization header).

credential_access.note: Pricing page advertises 'Start for free'. The Get Started docs (https://docs.attio.com/get-started) describe signup, dashboard onboarding and how to access API credentials, indicating developers can obtain credentials themselves after signup.

api_surface.note: The official API Overview states the Attio API is a RESTful API using standard HTTP methods over HTTPS with JSON responses, with a v2 base URL (https://api.attio.com/v2) and resources including Lists, Objects, Records, Notes, Tasks, and more—indicating a broad REST surface.

buildability.note: Public API docs exist and developers can obtain API keys via signup (free tier available). You can sign up for an account and generate API keys to start building.
